name: The natural pool of río Viejas from Castañar de Ibor — circular by the río Ibor
name_original: La piscina natural de río Viejas desde Castañar de Ibor — circular por el río Ibor
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Castañar De Ibor traverse a landscape characterized by the Villuercas Geopark, featuring significant elevation changes and river valleys. The region includes notable water bodies such as the Valdecañas Reservoir and the Ibor River. This area offers varied terrain, from challenging mountain passes to routes alongside natural pools, suitable for experienced road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Castañar De Ibor The most popular road cycling route is Villuercas Geopark: Arrebatacapas and Hospital Pass Loop, a 69.5 miles (111.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route features challenging climbs through the Villuercas Geopark. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Catañar de Ibor to Campillo de Deleitosa — circular, a difficult 41.4 miles (66.7 km) path. This circular route offers varied terrain between the two towns. Local road cyclists also love the Valdecañas Reservoir Loop, a 33.3 miles (53.5 km) trail leading through reservoir views, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Castañar De Ibor is defined by river valleys, reservoir circuits, and mountain passes. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Castañar De Ibor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Castañar De Ibor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Castañar De Ibor?
A: There are 13 road cycling routes around Castañar De Ibor, offering a variety of experiences from moderate to difficult. The region is known for its significant elevation changes and diverse terrain within the Villuercas Geopark.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Castañar De Ibor?
A: The road cycling routes around Castañar De Ibor are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, with no routes specifically marked as easy. Cyclists should be prepared for challenging terrain and elevation changes, characteristic of the Villuercas Geopark.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can tackle the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371640528/villuercas-geopark-arrebatacapas-and-hospital-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villuercas Geopark: Arrebatacapas and Hospital Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 69.5-mile (111.9 km) route featuring significant climbs. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371585498/catanar-de-ibor-to-campillo-de-deleitosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Catañar de Ibor to Campillo de Deleitosa — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 41.4 miles (66.7 km) with varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371585498/catanar-de-ibor-to-campillo-de-deleitosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Catañar de Ibor to Campillo de Deleitosa — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 41.4-mile (66.7 km) path. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058794665/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 33.3-mile (53.5 km) ride offering scenic views.

Q: What can I expect regarding the length and duration of road cycling routes?
A: Route lengths vary significantly. You'll find shorter, moderate rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058794665/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; at 33.3 miles (53.5 km), taking around 2 hours 48 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371640528/villuercas-geopark-arrebatacapas-and-hospital-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villuercas Geopark: Arrebatacapas and Hospital Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to 69.5 miles (111.9 km) and require over 5 hours to complete.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers numerous scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058794665/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides beautiful reservoir views. For mountain vistas, routes traversing the Villuercas Geopark, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4253507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arrebatachacas&lt;/a&gt;, offer impressive panoramas.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while road cycling?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera la Vieja (ruins)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5579771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañas del Castillo Castle&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark you could pass near.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Castañar De Ibor?
A: The region's climate, characterized by hot summers, suggests that spring and autumn are ideal for road cycling. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making the challenging climbs and longer routes more enjoyable.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Castañar De Ibor?
A: The road cycling routes in Castañar De Ibor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to mountain passes, and the stunning natural beauty of the Villuercas Geopark.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by natural pools or rivers?
A: Yes, the region is known for its water bodies. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353297923/the-natural-pool-of-rio-viejas-from-castanar-de-ibor-circular-by-the-rio-ibor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The natural pool of río Viejas from Castañar de Ibor — circular by the río Ibor&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a natural pool and follows the Ibor River, offering a refreshing experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Castañar De Ibor?
A: The terrain is quite varied, ranging from challenging mountain passes within the Villuercas Geopark to routes that follow river valleys and reservoir circuits. You should be prepared for significant elevation changes and a mix of ascents and descents.
tips: In the foothills of the Los Ibores region is Castañar de Ibor, on the road from Guadalupe to Navalmoral de la Mata.
Beautiful natural landscape of the Viejas river. Swimming wildly on a warm, sunny day in the natural pools.

waypoints.name: Natural Pool of the Viejas River
